title: astragalus asterias subsp. radiatus
intro: Astragalus asterias subsp. radiatus, also known as the radiate milkvetch, is a perennial plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its immunomodulatory properties, which help to regulate the immune system and prevent illness. The radiate milkvetch is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing stress and improving respiratory function. Astragalus asterias subsp. radiatus is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
